text: Sealing gaps around recessed lights rated for insulation: Materials needed: utility knife, caulk gun, expanding foam or caulk, foam board or fiberglass insulation, foil tape, gloves, mask, measuring tape. How to do it: identify air gaps or uninsulated surfaces, clean and dry the area, cut insulation or gasket material to size, apply sealant in continuous beads, press insulation into place without compressing it excessively, tape seams where appropriate, and recheck for drafts after curing

Removing and reinstalling the kitchen door stop: Materials needed: measuring tape, pencil, screwdriver, drill, level, anchors or screws rated for the surface, replacement part, utility knife, caulk or filler if needed. How to do it: clear the work area, mark the correct position, remove the old part if present, repair stripped holes or damaged backing, predrill pilot holes, fasten the part level and square, test its operation or load, fill visible gaps, and clean the area

Installing a trash pull-out cabinet: Materials needed: screwdriver, drill, measuring tape, level, pencil, appropriate screws, replacement hardware or wood parts, wood glue, clamps, sandpaper. How to do it: empty the cabinet, measure and mark locations carefully, remove the old or damaged component, predrill holes to prevent splitting, install the new hardware or part square and level, test movement and alignment, tighten fasteners, and touch up exposed wood or paint

Sealing gaps around a basement rim joist: Materials needed: utility knife, caulk gun, expanding foam or caulk, foam board or fiberglass insulation, foil tape, gloves, mask, measuring tape. How to do it: identify air gaps or uninsulated surfaces, clean and dry the area, cut insulation or gasket material to size, apply sealant in continuous beads, press insulation into place without compressing it excessively, tape seams where appropriate, and recheck for drafts after curing

Recaulking a bathtub edge: Materials needed: adjustable wrench, bucket, towels, plumber's tape, replacement seals or part, silicone caulk where needed, gloves. How to do it: shut off the water supply, relieve pressure by opening the fixture, place a bucket under the work area, remove the old part without forcing fittings, clean threads and mating surfaces, install the replacement with new seals, tighten snugly without over-torquing, turn water back on slowly, and check for leaks

Replacing a ceiling light fixture: Materials needed: non-contact voltage tester, screwdriver, wire labels, appropriate replacement device, wire nuts or connectors if required, electrical tape, safety glasses. How to do it: turn off power at the breaker, verify the circuit is de-energized with a tester, photograph and label existing connections, remove the old part, connect the replacement exactly according to its instructions and local code, secure wires in the box without pinching, reinstall the cover, restore power, and test; hire a licensed electrician for unfamiliar wiring or code-required work

Installing the kitchen curtain rod: Materials needed: measuring tape, pencil, screwdriver, drill, level, anchors or screws rated for the surface, replacement part, utility knife, caulk or filler if needed. How to do it: clear the work area, mark the correct position, remove the old part if present, repair stripped holes or damaged backing, predrill pilot holes, fasten the part level and square, test its operation or load, fill visible gaps, and clean the area

Patching a pantry door: Materials needed: cleaner, scraper, sanding block, patching compound or filler, putty knife, primer, finish paint or sealant, brush, rags, protective tape. How to do it: protect adjacent surfaces, clean away grease and dust, scrape loose material, apply filler in thin layers, sand smooth after drying, prime porous spots, apply finish in even coats, and allow proper curing before heavy use

Replacing a dimmer switch: Materials needed: non-contact voltage tester, screwdriver, wire labels, appropriate replacement device, wire nuts or connectors if required, electrical tape, safety glasses. How to do it: turn off power at the breaker, verify the circuit is de-energized with a tester, photograph and label existing connections, remove the old part, connect the replacement exactly according to its instructions and local code, secure wires in the box without pinching, reinstall the cover, restore power, and test; hire a licensed electrician for unfamiliar wiring or code-required work

Making a floating shelf: Materials needed: measuring tape, pencil, square, saw, drill, screws, wood glue, clamps, sandpaper, lumber or plywood, finish or paint. How to do it: measure the available space, sketch dimensions, cut pieces square, sand exposed edges, dry-fit the assembly, glue and clamp joints, drive screws into predrilled holes, check for level and square, fill holes, sand again, and apply a protective finish

Painting the basement fireplace surround: Materials needed: drop cloths, painter's tape, spackle, sanding sponge, primer, interior paint, angled brush, roller, tray, clean rags. How to do it: remove hardware or protect edges with tape, patch dents and sand smooth, wipe dust away, prime bare or stained areas, apply two thin coats while keeping a wet edge, remove tape before the final coat fully cures, and let the surface dry before replacing hardware
